title: [cross-file] lora_mutex vs rx_mutex ordering undefined
description:
Interaction: lora_l2.c:lora_mutex <-> lichen_l2.c:rx_mutex

Bug: The code documents tx_mutex before rx_mutex ordering (lichen_l2.c:255-261). But there's no documented ordering between lora_mutex (lora_l2.c) and rx_mutex (lichen_l2.c).

The lora_mutex is used in:
- lichen_lora_l2_init() line 498
- lichen_lora_l2_start() line 558
- lichen_lora_l2_stop() line 647
- rx_thread (for callback snapshot) lines 471-474
- lichen_lora_l2_set_rx_callback() line 1026

The rx_mutex is used in:
- lichen_l2_input() line 1656
- lichen_l2_enable() lines 1004-1051, 1140-1178
- lichen_peer_add() line 646
- lichen_peer_remove() line 723
- lichen_l2_iface_init() lines 1426-1432, 1548-1561

Cross-module interaction:
1. rx_thread runs lora_rx_callback (no lock) -> lichen_l2_input() -> acquires rx_mutex
2. RX thread also acquires lora_mutex for callback snapshot (lines 471-474) BEFORE releasing it and calling callback

So the flow is:
- lora_mutex acquired (line 471)
- lora_mutex released (line 474)
- callback invoked (line 477)
- rx_mutex acquired inside callback (line 1656)

This is safe because lora_mutex is released before rx_mutex is acquired. But if future code changes the callback to need lora_mutex (e.g., for TX while in RX callback), deadlock could occur.

Also: lichen_lora_l2_stop() at line 647 acquires lora_mutex. Then at line 678 releases it. Then joins the thread. The thread may be in lichen_l2_input() holding rx_mutex. This is fine.

But: lichen_lora_l2_deinit() at line 834 calls k_mutex_init(&lora_mutex) without holding it. Then at line 873 calls lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort() which inits rx_mutex. Order doesn't matter here since mutexes are being reset.

This is more of a documentation gap than a bug. The ordering is implicitly safe but not formally documented like tx_mutex/rx_mutex is.
close_reason: Already documented: lora_l2.c lines 499-513 contain a comprehensive LOCK ORDER INVARIANT comment (project-LICHEN-i1gk.45) that explicitly documents lora_mutex vs rx_mutex ordering. The comment explains: (1) lora_mutex is released BEFORE callback invocation, (2) callback acquires rx_mutex, (3) no ordering exists because locks never nest, (4) CROSS-MODULE INVARIANT documents TX constraints. No additional documentation needed.
